text: ist 6 interesting spots in Copenhagen as the potential destinations, as well as the reason for choosing them, the admission prices, and the opening days, and list 6 important “travel tips” about Copenhagen.
This is the home of the Danish royal family
Known for its modern architecture, it's a beautiful place to visit
it opens from Tuesday to Friday.
This statue, inspired by the character from Grimm's Fairy Tales, is one of the most iconic landmarks in Copenhagen.
If you're a foodie, this world-famous restaurant is a must-visit

This is a 17th-century astronomy tower, offering a stunning view of the city.
One of the most significant landmarks in Copenhagen, both historically and politically.
The type of power sockets used in Denmark are Type A and Type K. So, you might need to bring an adapter.
The official currency in Denmark is the Danish Krone (DKK). Most places do not accept euros.
Copenhagen has 6 metro lines which can take you anywhere in the city.
The weather in Copenhagen in December can go below -15 degrees Celsius. So, pack your warmest clothes if you're visiting in winter.
The official language is Danish, but English is widely spoken in Copenhagen.
Remember, the “hello” in Danish is “hej”, “thank you” is “farvel”, and “goodbye” is “tak”.
